findings.summary: execute_full_pipeline and execute_core_pipeline duplicate nearly identical stage-list construction logic
evidence: Lines 146-170: execute_full_pipeline builds a stages list with 10 entries including LLM stages
Lines 183-204: execute_core_pipeline rebuilds the same list with identical entries minus LLM stages
Both methods share identical boilerplate for Clean, Environment Setup, Infra Tests, Project Tests, Analysis, PDF Rendering, Validation, Copy Outputs
findings.suggestion: Extract a single _build_stages(include_llm: bool) method that returns the stage list; both public methods delegate to it with their respective flag value

findings.summary: get_config_as_env_vars is a zero-policy wrapper over get_config_as_dict that adds no seam value
evidence: Lines 264-269: get_config_as_env_vars calls get_config_as_dict with respect_existing=True as its only logic
The docstring says 'Thin wrapper over get_config_as_dict with respect_existing=True by default'
Callers could call get_config_as_dict directly with the same argument; the wrapper creates a second public name with no behavioral boundary
findings.suggestion: Remove get_config_as_env_vars and update callers to use get_config_as_dict(repo_root, respect_existing=True) directly, or give the wrapper a meaningful policy distinction

findings.summary: validate_placeholder_consistency calls findall and discards the result before iterating with finditer
evidence: Line 503: placeholder_pattern = re.compile(r'\{([^}]+)\}')
Line 504: placeholder_pattern.findall(content) — result assigned to nothing, immediately discarded
Line 522: for match in placeholder_pattern.finditer(content): — actual iteration happens here
findings.suggestion: Remove the dead findall call on line 504; it has no effect and creates a misleading read at a validation boundary

findings.summary: copy_final_deliverables contains an if branch that detects a condition and then does nothing (bare pass)
evidence: Lines 526-528: if combined_pdf_src.exists(): / # Use project-specific name / pass
Lines 529-532: else: block reassigns combined_pdf_src and combined_pdf_dst to legacy names

The pass branch means the if-block is structurally misleading — it appears to branch on existence but the 'found' path has no effect; the fallback runs regardless only when the first path does not exist
findings.suggestion: Restructure as: set combined_pdf_src to project-specific path; if it does not exist, reassign to legacy path. Eliminates the dead pass and makes the intent explicit in one linear flow

findings.summary: Inline line-number computation content[: match.start()].count('\n') + 1 repeated four times inside extract_links
evidence: Lines 77, 91, 101 (internal_links, external_links, file_refs dicts): each independently computes content[: match.start()].count('\n') + 1
The expression is not extracted to a local variable or helper despite being identical in all three branches
findings.suggestion: Compute line_num = content[:match.start()].count('\n') + 1 once at the top of the loop body and reference it in all three branch dicts

findings.summary: _should_validate_path encodes a 30+ item skip-list as inline string literals split across two separate lists
evidence: Lines 234-271: skip_patterns list with 30 hardcoded strings
Lines 311-319: separate example_indicators list with 6 more hardcoded strings performing the same skip intent
Both lists are checked with the same 'if pattern in path_ref' loop idiom; splitting them provides no abstraction benefit
findings.suggestion: Merge into a single module-level constant SKIP_PATH_PATTERNS and drive both checks from it; or move to a data file if the list continues to grow

findings.summary: logging_utils.py accumulates logger setup, context managers, progress formatting, resource monitoring, and LogAggregator in one file
evidence: Lines 87-206: logger setup and get_logger
Lines 215-321: context managers (log_operation, log_timing, log_function_call)
Lines 329-515: utility functions including log_resource_usage with a psutil import
Lines 565-end: LogAggregator class with its own message storage and report generation
The file re-exports symbols from logging_formatters, logging_helpers, logging_progress, and logging_constants — it acts as both a hub and an implementation file
findings.suggestion: Move LogAggregator to its own module (logging_aggregator.py); move resource-usage logging to logging_resources.py; keep logging_utils.py as a clean re-export facade with only get_logger and setup_logger as implementations

findings.summary: _should_validate_path mixes three distinct filtering concerns in one function body that grows by accretion
evidence: Lines 234-271: skip-list membership check (30 literal strings)
Lines 278-282: template-variable and URL detection (structural patterns)
Lines 287-318: malformed-path heuristics (character-level artifact detection)
Lines 311-319: a second, separate indicator list for the same skip intent
The function has no single statement of responsibility; each concern adds independent conditional branches
findings.suggestion: Split into three focused predicates: _is_known_placeholder(path), _is_malformed_path(path), _is_skip_listed(path); compose them in _should_validate_path with explicit early returns

findings.summary: execute_full_pipeline and execute_core_pipeline violate single-responsibility by encoding stage membership as two parallel method bodies instead of a parameterized design
evidence: Lines 135-170 and 172-204: both methods open with identical resume-guard, build nearly identical stage lists, and call self._execute_pipeline(stages)
The only structural difference is whether LLM stages appear; this is a boolean decision, not a design boundary
Adding a new stage requires editing both methods
findings.suggestion: Introduce a single _build_stage_list(include_llm: bool) and have both public methods call it; the methods themselves become one-liners that express intent only
